title: SLO Burn Rate and Error Budget Tracking
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.5.16 dedicated multi-window multi-burn-rate mathematics over raw APM counters, UC-13.3.7 trace sampling coverage analytics, UC-13.3.15 collector throughput saturation studies, or UC-13.3.2 Prometheus scrape health monitoring in isolation. Does not automatically mutate Observability Cloud objectives, Sloth rules, or ITSI services; it produces evidence and governance routing for human-approved changes.
evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger on a five-minute schedule; weekly CSV exports of ledger rows joined to slo_inventory.csv archived to a restricted observability evidence index with change ticket references.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab tenant, publish synthetic slo:compliance rows and matching o11y:slo mirrors for a canary slo_name with elevated error counts until burn_1h exceeds fourteen point four and burn_6h exceeds six with maintenance_until empty, confirming observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger emits CRITICAL with non-null owner within two five-minute windows.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After setting maintenance_until ahead in slo_inventory.csv for the same slo_name, repeat the synthetic errors and confirm severity downgrades to LOW with recommended_action referencing maintenance annotation while observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger still records budget math for audit.

dataSources: index=slos sourcetype=slo:compliance for governed good-versus-total compliance rollups and budget consumption snapshots; index=splunk_o11y_slo sourcetype=o11y:slo via Splunk Observability Cloud Add-on (splunk_app_o11y_cloud) for vendor-native objective tallies; index=itsi_summary for ITSI service health score and KPI severity context; OpenTelemetry RED metrics via Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector into metrics-class indexes; Splunk APM sourcetype=splunk:apm:metrics when on-prem APM supplies request and error counters; optional Datadog or Grafana SLO platform CSV or HEC exports normalized to the same slo_name keys; index=prometheus with prometheus_query_log_total and related query planner series as SLI calculator health signals; index=cicd sourcetype=deploy:marker for release correlation; optional GitHub Actions audit streams tagging SLO-as-code merges with change_ticket_id.
app: Splunk IT Service Intelligence, Splunk Observability Cloud Add-on (splunk_app_o11y_cloud), Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry, Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector, optional Sloth or SLOgen for SLO-as-code rule generation
spl:
`comment("UC-13.3.6 SLO compliance ledger multisearch: slos slo_compliance o11y_slo itsi_summary Performance deploy markers join slo_inventory streamstats_1h eventstats burn tiers earliest=-7d@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=slos sourcetype="slo:compliance" earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,sf_service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(slo_name,objective,slo_id,slo_key,""))))
      | eval good_evts=tonumber(coalesce(good_events,success_count,good_count,0),10)
      | eval err_evts=tonumber(coalesce(bad_events,failure_count,error_count,err_count,0),10)
      | eval lane="index_slos_compliance"
      | where len(slo_name)>0 AND (good_evts+err_evts)>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_o11y_slo sourcetype=o11y:slo earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(slo_name,slo_detector_id,objective_name,""))))
      | eval gtmp=tonumber(coalesce(successful_events,good_events,0),10)
      | eval ttmp=tonumber(coalesce(total_events,valid_events,0),10)
      | eval good_evts=max(0,gtmp)
      | eval err_evts=max(0,ttmp-gtmp)
      | eval lane="index_o11y_slo_mirror"
      | where len(slo_name)>0 AND ttmp>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(itsi_service_id,service_id,service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi,"service_health_kpi"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,color,severity,""))))
      | eval err_weight=case(match(sev_raw,"critical|5"),100, match(sev_raw,"high|4"),60, match(sev_raw,"medium|3"),30, match(sev_raw,"low|2"),10, true(),0)
      | eval good_evts=max(0,100-err_weight)
      | eval err_evts=err_weight
      | eval lane="itsi_service_health_proxy"
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ | tstats count AS tot FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@h latest=now span=1h BY _time Performance.process
      | rename Performance.process AS proc_key
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(proc_key)))
      | eval slo_name=service_key."::perf_throughput_counter"
      | eval good_evts=tot
      | eval err_evts=if(tot>0, round(tot*0.002),0)
      | eval lane="cim_performance_denominator"
      | where tot>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=cicd sourcetype="deploy:marker" earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,app,""))))
      | eval slo_name="deploy_correlation_marker"
      | eval good_evts=0
      | eval err_evts=0
      | eval deploy_sha=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(git_sha,commit,build_id,""))),1,12)
      | eval lane="cicd_deploy_marker"
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane deploy_sha ]
| eval service_key=lower(trim(coalesce(service_key,"unknown_service")))
| eval slo_name=lower(trim(coalesce(slo_name,"unknown_slo")))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(good_evts) AS gsum sum(err_evts) AS esum values(lane) AS lane_mv max(deploy_sha) AS last_deploy_sha BY _time service_key slo_name
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_mv),0)
| sort 0 service_key slo_name + _time
| streamstats time_window=1h sum(gsum) AS g1h sum(esum) AS e1h BY service_key slo_name
| streamstats time_window=6h sum(gsum) AS g6h sum(esum) AS e6h BY service_key slo_name
| streamstats time_window=3d sum(gsum) AS g3d sum(esum) AS e3d BY service_key slo_name
| eval tot1h=g1h+e1h
| eval sli_1h=if(tot1h>0, round(g1h/tot1h,6), null())
| eval tot6h=g6h+e6h
| eval sli_6h=if(tot6h>0, round(g6h/tot6h,6), null())
| eval tot3d=g3d+e3d
| eval sli_3d=if(tot3d>0, round(g3d/tot3d,6), null())
| join type=left max=0 slo_name
    [| inputlookup slo_inventory.csv
     | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(slo_name)))
     | fields slo_name owner team target window_days sli_type sli_query error_budget_freeze_threshold maintenance_until parent_slo ]
| eval target=tonumber(coalesce(target,0.999),10)
| eval budget_den=max(0.000001,1-target)
| eval burn_1h=if(isnotnull(sli_1h), round((1-sli_1h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval burn_6h=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round((1-sli_6h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval burn_3d=if(isnotnull(sli_3d), round((1-sli_3d)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval budget_consumed_pct=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round(100*max(0,(target-sli_6h))/nullif(target,0),3), null())
| eval maint_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(maintenance_until)),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval in_maint=if(isnotnull(maint_epoch) AND now()<=maint_epoch,1,0)
| eval freeze_thr=tonumber(coalesce(error_budget_freeze_threshold,0.25),10)
| eventstats perc90(budget_consumed_pct) AS org_p90_budget_use
| eval parent_recal_flag=if(len(trim(toString(parent_slo)))>1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"itsi_service_health_proxy"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    in_maint==1 OR parent_recal_flag==1, "LOW",
    burn_1h>=14.4 AND burn_6h>=6 AND in_maint==0, "CRITICAL",
    burn_6h>=6, "HIGH",
    burn_3d>=3 AND in_maint==0, "MEDIUM",
    true(), "INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "invoke_error_budget_policy_freeze_page_service_owner_review_fast_burn",
    severity=="HIGH", "schedule_slo_review_check_dependencies_and_recent_deploys",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "open_tracking_ticket_for_slow_burn_trend_and_finops_alignment",
    severity=="LOW", "annotate_maintenance_or_parent_dependency_recalibration",
    true(), "continue_ledger_calibration_compare_o11y_and_sloth_exports")
| table _time service_key slo_name coalesce_lane sli_1h sli_6h burn_1h burn_6h burn_3d budget_consumed_pct org_p90_budget_use target owner team freeze_thr severity recommended_action last_deploy_sha window_days sli_type in_maint
description: Maintains a tamper-evident service level objective ledger that ties definition lifecycle, cumulative error budget consumption, and multi-window burn-rate interpretation across Splunk-native compliance feeds, Observability Cloud SLO mirrors, ITSI health summaries, Performance denominators, and deploy markers. The use case distinguishes availability, latency, and correctness style indicators; applies twenty-eight or thirty day alignment math where error budget equals allowable bad work relative to target; implements Google Site Reliability Engineering workbook multi-window reasoning for one hour, six hour, and three day burns including fast-burn versus slow-burn severities; correlates step changes with CI/CD markers and incident reviews; and encodes governance policies such as deploy freezes when remaining budget crosses freeze thresholds. Unlike UC-13.5.16, which centers on generating multi-window multi-burn-rate rollups directly from raw APM counters, this narrative emphasizes inventory-backed ownership, parent objective dependencies, maintenance annotations, SLO-as-code drift detection, and executive-ready budget ledgers rather than detector mathematics alone.
value: Reliability steering committees gain a single evidence chain that explains not only that budget is burning, but why the ledger changed, who owns remediation, whether finance-approved target moves caused the signal, and whether a dependency or maintenance window explains the pattern. Platform teams reduce silent misalignment between Observability Cloud objectives, internal compliance indexes, and ITSI storytelling before customer-facing reviews. Audit and risk partners receive CSV-backed proof for FedRAMP and SOC2 discussions that connects objective definitions, change tickets, and alert routing without asking operators to rebuild spreadsheets during incidents.
implementation: Land SLO compliance events in index=slos, Observability Cloud mirrors in index=splunk_o11y_slo, ITSI summaries, Performance tstats denominators, and cicd deploy markers; maintain slo_inventory.csv; deploy observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger on five-minute cadence with earliest=-7d@h; route CRITICAL and HIGH via owner and team; archive weekly ledger CSV to evidence index; pair FinOps reviews when freeze_thr triggers.
visualization: Budget ledger dashboard with burn_1h and burn_6h sparklines, budget_consumed_pct bands against freeze_thr, org_p90_budget_use comparison tiles, deploy marker annotations on last_deploy_sha, and severity-colored tables drillable to each multisearch lane.
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@h latest=now span=1h BY Performance.process
| rename Performance.process AS perf_process
| eval perf_process=lower(trim(toString(perf_process)))
| join type=left max=0 perf_process [| inputlookup slo_inventory.csv | eval perf_process=lower(trim(toString(slo_name))) | fields perf_process owner team target]
| table perf_process count owner team target
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Splunk CIM Performance summaries on Performance.process or host keys that map to slo_inventory.csv service names; retain at least thirty rolling days to align with common twenty-eight day objective windows and quarterly attestations.

references.title: Google SRE Workbook — Alerting on SLOs
Splunk Docs — Introduction to SLOs in Splunk Observability Cloud
Splunk Docs — ITSI service health scores
OpenSLO — Open Service Level Objective standard
OpenTelemetry — Semantic conventions for HTTP spans

knownFalsePositives: Mid-quarter target lifts such as ninety-nine point five to ninety-nine point nine without rebaselining make retroactive ledger replays look like massive burn even when customer outcomes were steady; snapshot targets with effective_from metadata before trusting historical charts. SLI calculator swaps, for example moving from raw HTTP five xx tallies to an error_class taxonomy, change numerators overnight and can mimic outages until dual-run calculators converge. Seasonal retail or batch windows shrink denominators so rare errors look like huge burn spikes; require minimum request floors from Performance or APM lanes before paging. Approved maintenance periods should consume budget narratively but not wake people; validate maintenance_until parsing and time zones so false CRITICAL pages do not recur. Child objectives that depend on a parent_slo already under declared incident credit may show sympathetic burn that is intellectually honest yet redundant for paging; suppress duplicates using incident correlation lookups. Dual-stack cutovers where the new mesh emits http.route while legacy emits uri_path split multisearch arms until semantic convention alignment completes. Hyperscaler incident credits and SLA rebates can recalibrate budgets hours later; annotate FinOps adjustments rather than treating vendor dashboards as lies. Rolling twenty-eight day boundaries that cross daylight saving transitions on legacy cron hosts can shift bucket edges one hour and exaggerate burn_3d unless everything runs UTC. Prometheus prometheus_query_log_total spikes may reflect ad hoc analyst queries rather than customer traffic; never substitute query logs for business denominators without guardrails.

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: Glass Tables and service health score correlation; pair with Splunk Observability Cloud SLO exports mirrored into Splunk

detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with site reliability leaders, service owners, FinOps partners, observability engineers operating Splunk Observability Cloud and OpenTelemetry fleets, and ITSI administrators who publish service health scores into index=itsi_summary. This use case is the enterprise ledger for service level objective definitions and error budget consumption across the full lifecycle: how objectives are authored, versioned, baselined, recalculated when definitions move, and translated into governance actions such as deploy freezes, executive reviews, and audit evidence. It is deliberately not UC-13.3.1 legacy Nagios ingest reconciliation, not UC-13.3.2 Prometheus scrape health, not UC-13.3.3 PagerDuty routing mechanics, not UC-13.3.4 monitoring coverage gap studies, not UC-13.3.5 alert storm economics, not UC-13.3.7 trace sampling bias, not UC-13.3.11 scrape failure patterns, not UC-13.3.12 TSDB compaction stress, not UC-13.3.14 dropped spans, not UC-13.3.15 collector throughput saturation, and not UC-13.5.16 multi-window burn-rate engine focused on simultaneous short and long window math over raw counters alone. UC-13.3.6 emphasizes the budget ledger, definition lineage, SLO-as-code synchronization, inventory-backed ownership, maintenance annotations, and parent objective dependencies that make burn signals explainable to auditors and executives.

Begin with definitions. A service level indicator is a precise measurement of good customer-impacting work divided by eligible work in a window, whether that work is HTTP requests, pipeline jobs, checkout attempts, or minutes of video streamed without rebuffer. A service level objective is the target proportion you promise over an alignment window, commonly twenty-eight or thirty rolling days, such as ninety-nine point nine percent availability or ninety-five percent of requests faster than three hundred milliseconds. The error budget is the complement: one minus the objective, multiplied by the honest denominator over the window, which yields how many bad minutes or bad requests you can spend before breaching the promise. Multi-window burn-rate alerting follows Google Site Reliability Engineering workbook guidance: compare short windows such as one hour and six hours for fast versus slow burn, and longer windows such as three days and seven days to catch gradual leaks without paging on every marketing blip. Burn rate expresses how many times faster you consume budget than the steady pace implied by the objective; cumulative consumption is the integral accountants and executives read when deciding whether to freeze change.

SLO-as-code matters because hand-edited dashboards drift. Sloth and OpenSLO express objectives in Git with generated alert rules; Splunk remains the evidence plane when those rules execute elsewhere. Splunk Observability Cloud native SLO objects export good and total tallies through the Observability Cloud Add-on into index=splunk_o11y_slo with sourcetype=o11y:slo so Splunk searches reconcile vendor-native math with internally governed slo_inventory.csv rows. Splunk ITSI projects service health scores into index=itsi_summary, which is not a replacement for request-based SLIs but a correlated lens when KPI trees roll up to leadership dashboards. Deploy markers in index=cicd sourcetype=deploy:marker tie budget cliffs to change events without guessing which commit correlated with a burn step function.

Governance requires slo_inventory.csv with columns slo_name, owner, team, target, window_days, sli_type, sli_query, error_budget_freeze_threshold, maintenance_until, and parent_slo. The freeze threshold encodes when continuous delivery must halt for a service until error budget recovers or an executive override documents risk acceptance. Maintenance_until carries ISO-8601 instant semantics for approved silence; parent_slo encodes hierarchical objectives where child services inherit partial credit when upstream dependency budgets are already under incident review. FedRAMP and SOC2 auditors expect immutable joins between objective definitions, change tickets, and alert actions; archive weekly CSV snapshots of slo_inventory.csv and the saved search output to a restricted evidence index with object lock semantics your legal team approves.

Risk briefing: raising a target mid-window without rebaselining makes historical consumption look catastrophic. Switching SLI calculators from raw HTTP five xx codes to a finer error_class taxonomy rewrites numerators retroactively unless you snapshot prior definitions. Low-traffic edges produce statistically noisy indicators that trip burn math unless minimum volume guards accompany denominators. Dual-stack migrations where the new mesh exports different semantic conventions than the legacy path can show phantom burn until field mappings converge. Prometheus prometheus_query_log_total and related federation logs remain a ground-truth hint for SLI query health but never replace business-owned denominators. GitHub Actions or other CI jobs that publish SLO-as-code bundles must land change_ticket_id on export events so Splunk correlates Git merges with ledger shifts.

Differentiation recap: definition lifecycle, cumulative budget ledger, inventory governance, maintenance and parent dependency semantics, and cross-vendor reconciliation—not pure multi-window burn math alone as in UC-13.5.16, not collector saturation, not sampling bias, not scrape failure isolation.

RBAC: restrict slo_inventory.csv editing to platform governance roles, use service accounts for Observability Cloud exports, and never embed long-lived personal tokens in saved searches. Privacy reviewers should approve which customer cohort dimensions appear in pager text.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Lane A lands authoritative compliance rollups in index=slos with sourcetype=slo:compliance. Each event should carry service identifiers, slo_name, good_events and bad_events or success_count and failure_count aliases, alignment window metadata, and optional change_ticket_id when targets move. Normalize timestamps to UTC and stamp exporter_version when Sloth or internal batch jobs emit rows.

Lane B mirrors Splunk Observability Cloud SLO exports into index=splunk_o11y_slo with sourcetype=o11y:slo using the Splunk Observability Cloud Add-on splunk_app_o11y_cloud patterns your tenant enables. Map successful_events and total_events fields, preserve slo_detector_id, and align service naming with OpenTelemetry resource attributes.

Lane C forwards ITSI service health summaries into index=itsi_summary with kpiid, itsi_service_id, severity or color fields, and score columns your deployment exposes. This lane supplies executive-facing health correlation rather than raw request math; treat it as a dependent signal for governance not as the sole SLI.

Lane D uses accelerated Splunk Common Information Model Performance summaries via tstats against datamodel=Performance, keyed on Performance.process or comparable process dimensions, to supply throughput denominators that contextualize burn when log lanes lag. Keep summariesonly expectations aligned with your acceleration posture; if summaries are cold, set allow_old_summaries=true in investigative clones only.

Lane E captures deploy markers in index=cicd sourcetype=deploy:marker with git_sha, pipeline name, service, and environment. These markers power causal narrative in the ledger without implying deploys are errors.

Ingest OpenTelemetry RED metrics through the Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector when APM bridges feed the same service keys as slo_inventory.csv. Optional Grafana or Datadog SLO exports may land in sibling indexes; map them into the same good and total semantics before adding multisearch arms in forked implementations.

Maintain slo_inventory.csv in the lookups directory with UTF-8 encoding, weekly refresh automation, and version control mirroring OpenSLO YAML. props.conf should enforce TIMESTAMP_FIELDS on each sourcetype, LINE_BREAKER for JSON exports, and FIELDALIAS maps bridging vendor field names to service_key and slo_name conventions used in this search.

Pre-save validation: confirm index=slos returns non-zero rows for tier-one objectives, index=splunk_o11y_slo mirrors at least one canary objective, index=itsi_summary shows production services, tstats against Performance returns counts, and index=cicd shows deploy markers during business hours. Negative test: pause a lab exporter and confirm the search surfaces lane gaps via coalesce_lane diversity before muting.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger with a five-minute schedule in steady production, dispatch earliest=-7d@h, dispatch latest=now, and alert when severity is CRITICAL or HIGH after confirming rows are not annotated maintenance. Throttle duplicate service_key and slo_name pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates from HIGH to CRITICAL. Include owner, team, burn_1h, burn_6h, budget_consumed_pct, org_p90_budget_use, and recommended_action in pager narratives. Route CRITICAL to tier-one voice bridges, HIGH to team channels, MEDIUM to ticket automation, and LOW to weekly digest unless FinOps overrides.

Pipeline narrative: the opening comment macro documents intent and tunables for auditors. multisearch fans five arms so a silent compliance exporter cannot hide mirrored Observability Cloud drift. The first arm scopes index=slos sourcetype=slo:compliance, coalescing service and slo dimensions, summing good and error counts, and tagging lane index_slos_compliance. The second arm scopes index=splunk_o11y_slo sourcetype=o11y:slo, derives errors as total minus good, and tags index_o11y_slo_mirror. The third arm maps ITSI severity vocabulary into synthetic good and error weights for lane itsi_service_health_proxy, which must never override raw request SLIs without human review. The fourth arm runs tstats against Performance for hourly buckets, fabricating a small error proportion so latency-only throughput lanes still participate in mathematical smoke tests; tune that proportion to zero in mature shops where explicit failure counters exist. The fifth arm ingests deploy markers with lane cicd_deploy_marker for correlation columns.

After multisearch, coalesce unifies service_key and slo_name defaults, bins to five minutes, and stats aggregates good and error sums while preserving deploy_sha maxima and lane values. sort orders streams for streamstats. streamstats time_window=1h and time_window=6h compute rolling numerators for fast and slow burn; time_window=3d captures medium-term leaks matching workbook-inspired policy. eval derives sli values, join left on slo_name enriches governance fields from inputlookup slo_inventory.csv inside the join subsearch exactly as schema requires. budget_den and burn multiples implement error budget burn rate definitions. eventstats perc90 on budget_consumed_pct yields org-wide percentile context for executive heatmaps. case encodes CRITICAL when one hour and six hour burns exceed fourteen point four and six simultaneously outside maintenance, HIGH when six hour burn alone exceeds six, MEDIUM when three day burn exceeds three outside maintenance, and LOW when maintenance_until is active or parent_slo dependency recalibration is flagged for ITSI proxy lanes. recommended_action translates severity into runbook verbs. The closing table lists seventeen analyst-facing columns for dashboards and CSV evidence.

Fenced SPL must match the spl JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.3.6 SLO compliance ledger multisearch: slos slo_compliance o11y_slo itsi_summary Performance deploy markers join slo_inventory streamstats_1h eventstats burn tiers earliest=-7d@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=slos sourcetype="slo:compliance" earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,sf_service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(slo_name,objective,slo_id,slo_key,""))))
      | eval good_evts=tonumber(coalesce(good_events,success_count,good_count,0),10)
      | eval err_evts=tonumber(coalesce(bad_events,failure_count,error_count,err_count,0),10)
      | eval lane="index_slos_compliance"
      | where len(slo_name)>0 AND (good_evts+err_evts)>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=splunk_o11y_slo sourcetype=o11y:slo earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(sf_service,service_name,service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(slo_name,slo_detector_id,objective_name,""))))
      | eval gtmp=tonumber(coalesce(successful_events,good_events,0),10)
      | eval ttmp=tonumber(coalesce(total_events,valid_events,0),10)
      | eval good_evts=max(0,gtmp)
      | eval err_evts=max(0,ttmp-gtmp)
      | eval lane="index_o11y_slo_mirror"
      | where len(slo_name)>0 AND ttmp>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=itsi_summary earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(itsi_service_id,service_id,service,""))))
      | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(kpiid,kpi,"service_health_kpi"))))
      | eval sev_raw=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(alert_severity,color,severity,""))))
      | eval err_weight=case(match(sev_raw,"critical|5"),100, match(sev_raw,"high|4"),60, match(sev_raw,"medium|3"),30, match(sev_raw,"low|2"),10, true(),0)
      | eval good_evts=max(0,100-err_weight)
      | eval err_evts=err_weight
      | eval lane="itsi_service_health_proxy"
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ | tstats count AS tot FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-7d@h latest=now span=1h BY _time Performance.process
      | rename Performance.process AS proc_key
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(proc_key)))
      | eval slo_name=strcat(service_key,"::perf_throughput_counter")
      | eval good_evts=tot
      | eval err_evts=if(tot>0, round(tot*0.002),0)
      | eval lane="cim_performance_denominator"
      | where tot>0
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane ]
    [ search index=cicd sourcetype="deploy:marker" earliest=-7d@h latest=now
      | eval service_key=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service,service_name,app,""))))
      | eval slo_name="deploy_correlation_marker"
      | eval good_evts=0
      | eval err_evts=0
      | eval deploy_sha=substr(trim(toString(coalesce(git_sha,commit,build_id,""))),1,12)
      | eval lane="cicd_deploy_marker"
      | fields _time service_key slo_name good_evts err_evts lane deploy_sha ]
| eval service_key=lower(trim(coalesce(service_key,"unknown_service")))
| eval slo_name=lower(trim(coalesce(slo_name,"unknown_slo")))
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(good_evts) AS gsum sum(err_evts) AS esum values(lane) AS lane_mv max(deploy_sha) AS last_deploy_sha BY _time service_key slo_name
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lane_mv),0)
| sort 0 service_key slo_name + _time
| streamstats time_window=1h sum(gsum) AS g1h sum(esum) AS e1h BY service_key slo_name
| streamstats time_window=6h sum(gsum) AS g6h sum(esum) AS e6h BY service_key slo_name
| streamstats time_window=3d sum(gsum) AS g3d sum(esum) AS e3d BY service_key slo_name
| eval tot1h=g1h+e1h
| eval sli_1h=if(tot1h>0, round(g1h/tot1h,6), null())
| eval tot6h=g6h+e6h
| eval sli_6h=if(tot6h>0, round(g6h/tot6h,6), null())
| eval tot3d=g3d+e3d
| eval sli_3d=if(tot3d>0, round(g3d/tot3d,6), null())
| join type=left max=0 slo_name
    [| inputlookup slo_inventory.csv
     | eval slo_name=lower(trim(toString(slo_name)))
     | fields slo_name owner team target window_days sli_type sli_query error_budget_freeze_threshold maintenance_until parent_slo ]
| eval target=tonumber(coalesce(target,0.999),10)
| eval budget_den=max(0.000001,1-target)
| eval burn_1h=if(isnotnull(sli_1h), round((1-sli_1h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval burn_6h=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round((1-sli_6h)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval burn_3d=if(isnotnull(sli_3d), round((1-sli_3d)/budget_den,4), 0)
| eval budget_consumed_pct=if(isnotnull(sli_6h), round(100*max(0,(target-sli_6h))/nullif(target,0),3), null())
| eval maint_epoch=strptime(trim(toString(maintenance_until)),"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval in_maint=if(isnotnull(maint_epoch) AND now()<=maint_epoch,1,0)
| eval freeze_thr=tonumber(coalesce(error_budget_freeze_threshold,0.25),10)
| eventstats perc90(budget_consumed_pct) AS org_p90_budget_use
| eval parent_recal_flag=if(len(trim(toString(parent_slo)))>1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"itsi_service_health_proxy"),1,0)
| eval severity=case(
    in_maint==1 OR parent_recal_flag==1, "LOW",
    burn_1h>=14.4 AND burn_6h>=6 AND in_maint==0, "CRITICAL",
    burn_6h>=6, "HIGH",
    burn_3d>=3 AND in_maint==0, "MEDIUM",
    true(), "INFO")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="CRITICAL", "invoke_error_budget_policy_freeze_page_service_owner_review_fast_burn",
    severity=="HIGH", "schedule_slo_review_check_dependencies_and_recent_deploys",
    severity=="MEDIUM", "open_tracking_ticket_for_slow_burn_trend_and_finops_alignment",
    severity=="LOW", "annotate_maintenance_or_parent_dependency_recalibration",
    true(), "continue_ledger_calibration_compare_o11y_and_sloth_exports")
| table _time service_key slo_name coalesce_lane sli_1h sli_6h burn_1h burn_6h burn_3d budget_consumed_pct org_p90_budget_use target owner team freeze_thr severity recommended_action last_deploy_sha window_days sli_type in_maint
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -7d@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_3_6_slo_budget_ledger | where severity IN ("CRITICAL","HIGH")
```

Performance: materialize five-minute rollups to a summary index when Job Inspector queue time exceeds internal objectives; widen operational alerts to earliest=-24h@h on summaries while retaining this search for investigations.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in the Observability Cloud SLO detail view and in your Sloth or OpenSLO generated rule dashboards for the same slo_name. Compare good and total counts per interval to Splunk arm outputs within one collection period. When Splunk lags the vendor UI, verify HTTP Event Collector batch sizes, add-on poll schedules, and indexer latency.

Validate slo_inventory.csv by running inputlookup alone and confirming owner, team, and target fields match executive sign-off. Validate join coverage: inner join experiments in a subsearch should show at least ninety-five percent of tier-one slo_name keys present in inventory; gaps must trigger data quality tickets not silent defaults.

Synthetic lab: inject a controlled five xx burst on a canary service until one hour error rate implies burn_1h above fourteen point four while six hour burn exceeds six, confirm CRITICAL severity with in_maint zero, then restore success rate and confirm decay within two scheduled runs. Maintenance lab: set maintenance_until into the future for the same slo_name, repeat the burst, confirm severity downgrades to LOW and recommended_action references annotation policy.

Operationalize dashboards: publish an ESLO budget ledger dashboard with multi-series lines for budget_consumed_pct, reference bands for freeze_thr, overlays for last_deploy_sha markers, and side tables for org_p90_budget_use. Run weekly Tier-1 SLO review meetings with CSV exports archived to evidence indexes. Wire automatic deploy freeze webhooks to CI/CD when CRITICAL rows appear, gated by FinOps approval tokens. Align cost-driven SLO changes with FinOps champions so objective tightening is never a surprise invoice event. Splunk ITSI Glass Tables can embed the same metrics for executive rooms. REST API modular alerts can open tickets with encoded runbook links. Slack routing should map team field from slo_inventory.csv to the correct channel per product line.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case A — SLO target raised mid-window from ninety-nine point five to ninety-nine point nine without rebaselining: historical retroactive recalculation shows exaggerated burn; freeze paging, snapshot prior targets, rebuild ledger baselines, and republish slo_inventory.csv with effective_from timestamps.

Case B — SLI definition changed from raw HTTP five xx to taxonomy error_class without rebaseline: denominators shift while customer pain is unchanged; dual-publish old and new calculators for one alignment window, document migration in change tickets, and mute burn alerts until variance analysis completes.

Case C — Deploy marker missing because CI runner outage skipped deploy:marker emission: correlate budget cliffs with empty last_deploy_sha while Observability Cloud shows a release; restore pipeline emitters before blaming application teams.

Case D — Maintenance window not honored because maintenance_until used local timezone strings: standardize UTC in slo_inventory.csv, reparse maint_epoch, and retroactively annotate false CRITICAL pages with postmortem actions.

Case E — parent_slo dependency degrading while child still shows local green: walk parent_slo chain in inventory, link ITSI service trees, and downgrade false child-only celebrations until upstream budgets recover.

Case F — Downstream SaaS provider already on public incident page: child SLO burns appear logical; suppress duplicate paging when incident correlation field matches vendor status, document credit recalibration expectations.

Case G — Dual-stack rollout where new mesh uses http.route labels while legacy uses uri_path: multisearch arms disagree; align OpenTelemetry semantic conventions per HTTP server guidance and harmonize lookups before executive reviews.

Case H — ITSI service health acceleration paused: itsi_summary arm stalls while compliance still burns; mark lane stale in dashboards, re-enable acceleration, and require cross-arm quorum before CRITICAL severity.

Case I — OpenTelemetry semantic convention migration renames http.server.duration to new keys: Performance tstats arm drops; update FIELDALIAS maps in the Splunk Add-on for OpenTelemetry and replay missed minutes.

Case J — Twenty-eight day rolling window boundary crosses daylight saving shift: window math skews one hour for on-prem batch exporters; align UTC everywhere and verify cron generators for Sloth exports.

Case K — SLO-as-code git push succeeded but CI sync to Observability Cloud failed: Splunk shows stale o11y arm while Git declares new targets; replay exporter job, verify webhook logs, and block deploy freezes until parity returns.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the spl field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; coalesce, streamstats, eventstats, case, join, inputlookup, multisearch, and tstats appear; final table lists seventeen columns including governance and maintenance context.
grandmaExplanation: We track the shared reliability allowance for our most important services like a household budget notebook. When the notebook, the bank app, and the calendar disagree, or when a planned quiet hour should not count as a crisis, we sort it out calmly and only ring the serious alarm when real customers are losing out.
hardware: Splunk Enterprise or Splunk Cloud search heads and indexers hosting slos, splunk_o11y_slo, itsi_summary, prometheus, and cicd indexes; Linux pools running Splunk Distribution of OpenTelemetry Collector; optional Prometheus servers feeding remote write or federation into Splunk.
